notes: 64-bit RISC processor architecture; key DEC differentiator. IP sold to Intel 2001; last Alpha systems sold by HP until 2007.
DIGITAL's Telecommunications Management Platform; supports TMN standards; proactive fault detection. Product continued under HP/Compaq as HP OpenView extensions.
Enterprise telecom billing platform. 50% faster on Alpha vs other platforms per benchmark. Kenan acquired by Lucent 1999; Amdocs acquired Kenan billing assets from Lucent in 2015.
Store-and-forward SMS text messaging platform; European GSM standard support. Became foundational to global SMS ecosystem. EMI protocol used by CMG.
Software routing phone calls over IP/Intranet to eliminate toll charges. TAPI-compatible. Acquired by Nokia 1998; technology integrated into Nokia gateway products.
US version of GSM at 1900 MHz band. CMG targeted PCS 1900 for SMS services. Succeeded by 3G/4G/5G standards.
High-end symmetric multiprocessing Alpha server used in telco deployments. Succeeded by Compaq/HP server lines.
DEC's proprietary OS; ran on Alpha. Still maintained by VMS Software Inc. (VSI) under HP license; used in niche mission-critical deployments.
